zone_6b_notes: Hardy to Zone 5 â€” reliable in Zone 6b Farmington with tip dieback in harsh winters; cut to within 6 inches of ground in early spring before new growth emerges. Black Knight is the classic deep purple cultivar with intensely fragrant 12-inch flower spikes. Blooms July through frost on new growth â€” the hard annual pruning ensures vigorous flowering. Monarch butterflies, swallowtails, and hummingbirds visit constantly during bloom. Note: standard Buddleja davidii is invasive in some regions; sterile or low-seed varieties like 'Miss Molly' or 'Lo and Behold' series are preferred where this is a concern. In Missouri, it rarely naturalized seriously but self-seeding can occur.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant nursery stock in spring after last frost or in fall

water: moderate â€” drought tolerant once established
